name: Perfect Chocolate Cake/Mccalls
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine cocoa with boiling water and whisk until smooth. Cool completely. Sift flour with soda, salt, and baking powder. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease three 9 by 1 1/2 inch layer-cake pans well.
2. In a large bowl of electric mixer, beat butter and sugar at high speed until light (about 5 minutes). Alternately add flour mixture (in fourths) and cocoa mixture (in thirds), beginning and ending with flour mixture. Do not overbeat. Divide batter evenly into pans and smooth tops.
3. Bake 25 to 30 minutes, or until the surface springs back when gently pressed with a fingertip.
4. Cool in pans for 10 minutes. Loosen sides with a spatula, then remove cakes from pans and cool on racks.
Frosting:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine chocolate pieces, cream, and butter. Stir over medium heat until smooth. Remove from heat and blend in confectioners' sugar with a whisk. Set bowl over ice (using ice cubes) and beat until it holds shape.
2. Whip cream with sugar and vanilla; refrigerate.
Assembling the Cake:
1. Place one layer on a plate, top side down. Spread with half of the cream.
2. Place second layer, top side down, and spread with remaining cream.
3. Place third layer, top side up, on top of the cake.
4. Frost the sides first, covering the whipped cream, then use the rest of the frosting to frost the top, swirling decoratively.
Refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving. To cut, use a thin-edged sharp knife and slice with a sawing motion. Enjoy your perfectly frosted chocolate cake!
